description_long: This is a medium-sized wooden barrel with a classic cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and tapering towards the top and bottom. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, each displaying a natural wood grain texture and subtle knots, giving it a rustic appearance. Two wide, dark metal hoops encircle the barrel, one near the top and one near the bottom, each fastened with visible round rivets for structural reinforcement. The top of the barrel is open, revealing a hollow interior, and the rim is uneven, suggesting wear or hand-crafting. The wood appears untreated or only lightly finished, contributing to its matte, slightly rough surface. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, storage yards, or parks, often used for storage, decoration, or waste collection.
description: A rustic wooden barrel with metal hoops, open at the top and featuring a natural wood grain texture. The barrel is reinforced with dark metal bands and has a slightly bulging, cylindrical form.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open top, wood grain, and two prominent metal hoops with visible rivets.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curvature of the barrel and the alignment of the metal hoops and rivets.
description_view_2: Another side view with a clear look at the wood texture and the even spacing of the metal bands.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the continuous wood staves and the metal hoops, with less visible rivet detail.
